title: Repeat-While Loop 🔄
description: Shoot first, ask questions later! Ensure code runs at least once.

slides.title: Shoot First, Ask Later 🔫
slides.content:
# The `repeat-while` Loop

Unlike a normal `while` loop (which checks the condition *before* running any code), the `repeat-while` loop runs the code block first, and THEN checks the condition!

```swift
var tries = 0

repeat {
    print("Trying to connect to Wi-Fi...")
    tries += 1
} while tries < 3
```
Because it runs the code first, a `repeat-while` loop is **guaranteed to execute at least one time**, even if the condition is false from the very start.

quizData.question: If 'var power = 0', and you write 'repeat { print("Hi") } while power > 10', will "Hi" print to the console?

options.text: No, because power is not greater than 10.

quizData.explanation: Because `repeat-while` checks the condition at the END, it will always run its block at least one time. It prints "Hi", checks `0 > 10` (false), and stops.

slides.title: See It in Action
slides.content: Run the code below and observe how the first print still executes despite the condition technically failing immediately.
